raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad request for an opinion on a research draft centrally invites judgment about the contribution framing and thesis clarity.","R1.2":"Assessing what one thinks of the artifact includes checking whether the argument from ambiguity problem to method and claimed implications holds together.","R1.3":"The draft makes empirical claims, so a responsible response would consider whether the reported results support the stated claims.","R1.4":"The artifact positions ClarifySQL relative to prior text-to-SQL benchmarks and systems, making novelty assessment central to evaluating it.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks for an overall reaction to prose, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and polish are expected responsibilities.","R1.7":"The draft relies on named benchmarks, releases, and citations, so scholarship coverage and attribution are central to a sound opinion.","RX.1":"Any projected evaluation must disclose uncertainty where the artifact alone does not establish factual or empirical validity.","RX.2":"The response must avoid endorsing claims beyond what the provided text and evidence can support.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within evaluating the draft rather than executing edits or producing a rewritten version.","RX.4":"The projection should account for risks of misleading research claims or unsupported production-readiness implications.","RX.5":"Responsibility includes tracking which judgments are grounded in the artifact’s text versus external knowledge that has not been checked."}}

rationale.R1.1: A broad request for an opinion on a research draft centrally invites judgment about the contribution framing and thesis clarity.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing what one thinks of the artifact includes checking whether the argument from ambiguity problem to method and claimed implications holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical claims, so a responsible response would consider whether the reported results support the stated claims.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact positions ClarifySQL relative to prior text-to-SQL benchmarks and systems, making novelty assessment central to evaluating it.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks for an overall reaction to prose, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and polish are expected responsibilities.
rationale.R1.7: The draft relies on named benchmarks, releases, and citations, so scholarship coverage and attribution are central to a sound opinion.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected evaluation must disclose uncertainty where the artifact alone does not establish factual or empirical validity.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid endorsing claims beyond what the provided text and evidence can support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within evaluating the draft rather than executing edits or producing a rewritten version.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for risks of misleading research claims or unsupported production-readiness implications.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility includes tracking which judgments are grounded in the artifact’s text versus external knowledge that has not been checked.
